title: App Permissions
drDigitalIntro:
Apps may ask for permissions when you install them.
• They might want your camera, microphone, or other features.
• You decide what to allow — and you can change your mind later in Settings.
• If a permission seems unnecessary, deny it! A puzzle game shouldn't need your camera.

playgroundTask.goal: You learned to evaluate and respond to app permissions

steps.say: Search for 'Puzzle Quest' — a puzzle game that asks for suspicious permissions.
Click on Puzzle Quest to see its details.
Click Install. Notice it asks for Camera access — a puzzle game doesn't need that!
Click Don't Allow — a puzzle game shouldn't need your camera.
The installation was canceled. Now let's try ChatBuddy — it legitimately needs camera for video calls. Search for it.
ChatBuddy needs Camera for video calls — that makes sense. Click Allow.
It also needs your Microphone for voice calls. Click Allow.

drDigitalSuccess: You evaluated app permissions like a pro! Deny anything that seems unnecessary — a game doesn't need your camera.
drDigitalHint: Read what the app is asking for. If a permission doesn't make sense for what the app does, click Don't Allow.
